# Pull Request

## Summary
Briefly describe what this PR does and which release blocker or goal it addresses.

## Acceptance criteria mapping
- [ ] Criterion 1 – explanation of how it’s satisfied.
- [ ] Criterion 2 – ...
- [ ] Criterion 3 – ...

## Changed behavior or functionality
Describe the changes made. Include before/after where relevant.

## Tests added or updated
List any unit, integration, or e2e tests added or modified. Include links or file names.

## Risk classification
- Initial risk label: low/medium/high/unknown
- Does this PR modify any high‑risk areas (auth, DB, billing, secrets, production config, workflows, CODEOWNERS, AGENTS.md)? If yes, add `high-risk` label and request human approval.

## Validation results
Summarize the results of typecheck, lint, unit tests, integration tests, e2e tests, build, visual tests, and other required checks. Provide links to CI runs if applicable.

## Remaining risks and open questions
Identify any known gaps, untested areas, or follow-up work.

## Rollback and migration
Describe how to roll back this change if needed. List any migrations and how to undo or forward fix them.

## Auto-merge eligibility
- All required checks pass: yes/no
- P0/P1 review issues resolved: yes/no
- Risk classification: low-risk / medium-risk / high-risk
- Auto-merge enabled: yes/no (only for low-risk PRs)
